annotations.text: The bi vector generated by the outer product of two vectors called v one and v two

annotation.text: The two vectors v one and v two, which appear in the accompanying diagram, form a parallelogram
The vector v one and its translate form two opposite sides of the parallelogram
The first of the two vectors v one
The translate of v one by v two forms the side of the parallelogram opposite v one
In the same way, the vector v two and its translate form two opposite sides of the parallelogram
The translate of v two by v one forms the side of the parallelogram opposite v two
The two vectors and their translates form four sides of a parallelogram
The ordering of the two vectors induces an orientation on the parallelogram that is denoted by this arrow
